contentBlocks.title: Why convert HTML to Markdown?
contentBlocks.html:
HTML is powerful but difficult to read and edit without specialized tools. Markdown uses simple syntax — # Heading instead of <h1>, **bold** instead of <strong>. It is the standard format for GitHub/GitLab documentation, static site generators (Hugo, Jekyll, Gatsby, Astro), note-taking apps (Notion, Obsidian), and technical writing.

Converting HTML to Markdown is essential when migrating content from a CMS (WordPress, Joomla) to static site generators, moving articles to Git repositories, creating technical documentation, and archiving web content in a readable plain-text format.

The converter recognizes semantic HTML tags: headings (h1–h6), lists (ul, ol), links (a), images (img), code blocks (pre, code), tables, and text formatting (strong, em, del). It ignores irrelevant tags (div, span, script, style).

All processing happens locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server. No registration, no limits, no tracking.

contentBlocks.title: How does HTML to Markdown conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
HTML uses tags like <h1>, <strong>, <a>, and <ul> to structure content. Markdown uses simpler symbols: # for headings, ** for bold, [text](url) for links, and - for lists. The converter parses the HTML document tree and maps each element to its Markdown equivalent.

For example, <h1>Title</h1> becomes # Title, <strong>bold</strong> becomes **bold**, and <a href="url">link</a> becomes [link](url). Complex structures like tables, code blocks, and nested lists are also handled.

Converting HTML to Markdown is useful for migrating website content to documentation systems, static site generators, or any platform that uses Markdown. The conversion runs entirely in your browser — no data is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: Tips for converting HTML to MARKDOWN
contentBlocks.description: A few tips to help you avoid common issues during conversion:

items.title: Inline styles are removed
items.description: Markdown does not support CSS styling. Inline styles, classes, and IDs in your HTML are stripped during conversion. Only the semantic structure and text content are preserved.

items.title: Complex layouts
items.description: HTML layouts using divs, grids, or flexbox cannot be represented in Markdown. The converter extracts the text content and preserves headings, lists, and links.

items.title: Images and links
items.description: HTML <img> tags become ![alt](src) and <a> tags become [text](url) in Markdown. Relative URLs are preserved as-is.

items.title: Tables
items.description: HTML tables are converted to Markdown pipe tables where possible. Complex tables with colspan or rowspan may be simplified.

contentBlocks.title: What HTML elements are converted?
contentBlocks.html:
The converter handles all common semantic HTML elements:

  • Headings – <h1> through <h6> → # through ######
  • Text formatting – <strong>/<b> → **bold**, <em>/<i> → *italic*, <del> → ~~strikethrough~~
  • Links – <a href> → [text](url)
  • Images – <img> → ![alt](src)
  • Lists – <ul>/<ol> → - / 1. list items
  • Code – <code> → `inline`, <pre><code> → fenced blocks
  • Tables – <table> → pipe-separated Markdown tables

Non-semantic elements (div, span, script, style) are stripped. Only meaningful content is preserved in the Markdown output.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about HTML to Markdown conversion

items.question: Is converting HTML to Markdown free?
items.answer: Yes, this converter is completely free with no limits. No registration, no watermarks.

items.question: Does the converter upload my files?
items.answer: No. All processing happens locally in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes, the converter supports batch conversion. Add multiple files and convert them all simultaneously.

items.question: Does the converter work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes, the converter works on any device with a modern web browser, including smartphones and tablets.

items.question: Is converting HTML to Markdown safe?
items.answer: Yes. The entire conversion runs locally in your browser. Your data is never sent to any server. When you close the page, all data is automatically cleared from memory.

items.question: Will I lose formatting during conversion?
items.answer: Markdown supports headings, bold, italic, links, images, lists, code blocks, blockquotes, and tables. HTML-only features like custom CSS, colors, and complex layouts cannot be represented in Markdown and will be simplified.

items.question: Can I use the Markdown output in GitHub or GitLab?
items.answer: Yes. The output follows standard Markdown syntax compatible with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Notion, Obsidian, and any other platform that renders Markdown.
